directive_executed.title: Author PROP-063 — closed-issues.json ID-collision class (29 duplicate IDs) and decider next-ID intake hardening

All 4 directive-requested steps completed in the PROP: Step 1 per-collision class diagnosis (29 IDs classified A/B/C/A_hybrid/B+C), Step 2 structural fix design (4 changes: 2a allocator hardening, 2b decider-intake cross-reference, 2c daily integrity audit, 2d optional same-run guard), Step 3 retroactive dedup recommendations (per-ID disposition table with operator-minute estimates), Step 4 operator-cost vs benefit analysis (~30 min Phase 1, ~105 min Phase 2, recommended phased approach).
directive_executed.directive_status_action: Marked DIRECTIVE-20260528-001 status=completed with completed_at and completed_by_run per CLAUDE.md DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.

backlog_trend.false_positive_note: WoW computation matched 'major' threshold because the from-side reference open_issues_total was null (or 0) — divide-by-near-zero artifact. Absolute open_issues_total=1 is HEALTHY (lowest backlog observed in the queue-history). Threshold tripping is mechanical; the report flags it as info-with-false-positive rather than major. Suggest tinker-cost-engineering tweak: only apply WoW growth threshold when prior-week baseline ≥ 10 (else the relative-growth signal is meaningless).

previous_followup.context: PROP-062 was authored at tinker-2026-05-28T02-46 (per PROP-063 authored_by reference). PROP-061, PROP-060, and PROP-059 have been implemented per their  fields. PROP-063 is the natural follow-up to PROP-062's failure_pattern_d. Once PROP-063 Phase 1 lands, the integrity audit's first run will be the verification signal.

findings.description: Backlog-trend WoW growth tripped 'major' tier mechanically but the absolute count is 1 open issue — false positive due to near-zero baseline. Open queue is in the healthiest state observed.
findings.evidence: queue-history.jsonl appended this run; open_issues_total=1, closed_issues_velocity_7d=37, net_velocity_7d=+36.
findings.recommendation: Cost-engineering tweak: WoW growth thresholds (info/moderate/major) should only fire when prior-week baseline ≥ 10. Without this guard, the threshold turns noisy at the end of a successful burndown. Author a small PROP if it recurs.

findings.evidence: df -h /: 9.6G total, 9.4G used, 111M avail, 99% use. du -sm /sessions/eloquent-loving-carson/mnt /tmp/*-clone: 243 MB FUSE + 230 MB edit-clone + 231 MB tinker-clone + 10 MB /tmp/dome-*. /tmp/edit-clone is operator-owned (2h old, under 24h cleanup empowerment).
findings.recommendation: End-of-run rm -rf /tmp/tinker-clone (mandatory per PROP-060 FND-01) will reclaim ~290 MB. Operator clone (/tmp/edit-clone) is fresh — leave alone.
